title: Vermilion v1.0.0 Web Rebuild
content:
Vermilion has been completely rebuilt as a modern web application, migrating from its legacy Tkinter desktop interface to a unified React 19 + TypeScript + Python HTTP API architecture running on port 8100. This alignment ensures Vermilion matches the UX, performance, and styling conventions of the sibling Millionfold utility.

What changed in this transition:

  • Brutalist UI Layout: Replaced the old Tkinter view panels with a responsive off-white brutalist dashboard featuring tab layouts for options, naming rules, filters, and presets.
  • Clean Monorepo & Legacy Archival: Relocated all old Tkinter components (app.py, main.py) into a designated vermilion-legacy/ directory for clean preservation.
  • No Image Preview Bloat: Removed the image preview panel from the right column, leaving the workspace focused exclusively on execution progress logs and undo action panels.
  • Dry-Run Directory Planner: Generates a full tree view preview of the target filesystem modifications, showing folder allocations, file sizes, non-image separations, and naming outputs before committing changes.
  • Dynamic Renaming Templates: Drag-and-drop or manually order naming blocks including original basenames, custom label text, padded sequences, and date stamps.
  • Real-Time SSE Progress: Connects to a background execution worker using Server-Sent Events to report file copy/move progress, stats, and error detail lists in real time.
  • Operate and Reverse: Performs moves or copies on disk with automated conflict resolution strategies, and logs execution paths to allow clean, recursive Undo rollbacks.
  • Filesystem Browser Modal: Custom pop-up browser listing logical drives and remote subdirectories with in-place folder creation support.
